category_title: Students who exhibit verbal outbursts when frustrated by prescriptive reading tasks
minimal_context: Janice is asked to complete district-mandated reading activities during her Read 180 class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Janice, please begin the district-mandated reading activities for today's Read 180 class.

STUDENT: I'm not participating in this.

TUTOR: You need to complete the assigned reading.

STUDENT: I'm not doing it. You're just treating me like a behavior problem instead of a learner.

TUTOR: If you keep refusing to participate, I will have to call security to escort you to the office.

STUDENT: (Starts screaming) The only reason this happens is because I do have anger issues! But I just don't seem to get mad in history because she doesn't make me mad!

options.A: Help the student focus on learning by asking questions about the reading and ensuring she gets all the details.
options.B: Touch the student on the shoulder while she is in the district-mandated reading class.
options.C: Mention reading or enjoying specific books while she is in the district-mandated reading class.
options.D: Respond to the student by saying, 'Another one already? No way! I’m so proud of you! Good job, keep going.'
